title: 1.1:  Tests that set_margins() with bottom margin only results in content moving to history buffer when scrolling beyond the bottom margin
1.2: Tests that set_margins() with top margin only results in first line preservation and scrolling occurring only within the margin region
1.3: Tests that set_margins() with both top and bottom margins results in content outside the scroll region being preserved and scrolling occurring only within the defined margin boundaries
2.1: Places the test for bottom margin scrolling behavior in kitty_tests/screen.py
2.2: Places the test for top margin scrolling behavior in kitty_tests/screen.py
2.3: Places the test for combined top and bottom margin scrolling behavior in kitty_tests/screen.py
3.1: All tests use Python unittest framework with self.ae() assertion method for equality checks
3.2: All tests follow the naming pattern test_<description>
4.1: Follows the pattern of verifying screen buffer state after scrolling operations using appropriate assertion methods (e.g., linebuf/historybuf method)
4.2: Each margin test configures scroll region with set_margins(top, bottom) before executing draw and linefeed operations
